comments.text: As far as I can tell, the best result without any further restrictions on $G$ comes from this paper by Dean and Kouider, where they show that $\left \lfloor \frac{2}{3}n \right \rfloor$ edge-disjoint paths suffice. I found various sources claiming that Yan obtained the same upper bound of $\left \lfloor \frac{2}{3}n \right \rfloor$ in their PhD thesis titled On path decompositions of graphs, but I couldn't find it online. This result even holds for disconnected graphs by the way, in which case it is actually optimal. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

L. Pyber in [Py85] has proved this conjecture for graphs whose even-degree vertices induce a forest. X. Geng, M. Fang and D. Li in [GFL15] have proved this conjecture for a maximal outerplanar graph or a 2-connected outerplanar graph. M. Bonamy and T. J. Perrett in [BoPe19] have proved this conjecture for graphs with maximum degree at most 5. A. Blanche, M. Bonamy and N. Bonichon in [BBB21] have proved this conjecture for planar graphs. Y. Chu, G. Fan and Q. Liu in [CFL21] have proved this conjecture for graphs with maximum degree at most 6 in which the vertices of maximum degree form an independent set (with some exceptions). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
